topic: How The Moon Stabilizes Earth's Tilt
title: Why Earth's Seasons Stay Relatively Steady

lesson.learn: Earth's tilt is what gives us seasons, but the Moon helps keep that tilt from wandering too wildly. The Moon's gravity stabilizes Earth's spin axis by reducing the amplitude of long-term wobble. Without that stabilizing effect, Earth's axial tilt could change much more dramatically over millions of years.
lesson.simpler: The Moon helps keep Earth's axis from wobbling too much, which keeps our seasons more stable.
lesson.keyTakeaway: The Moon stabilizes Earth's tilt by acting as a gravitational brake on large axial swings.

thirdImageCredit.creator: Kevin W. Miller (Global Science and Technology, Inc.)

lesson.deeperDive: Earth's tilt is influenced by the Sun, the Moon, and the gravitational pull of the rest of the Solar System. The Moon matters because it increases the rate at which Earth's spin axis precesses, helping to keep the planet away from large chaotic swings in obliquity. This stability is important because large tilt variations would dramatically redistribute sunlight across the globe. In the long run, the Moon helps make Earth's climate more predictable than it would be otherwise.
lesson.funFact: Some planetary systems may be far less stable than Earth because they lack a large moon to steady their axial tilt.
